{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /config/.claude/skills/sc-task-unified-protocol\n\n> **NOTE**: Classification has already been performed by the `/sc:task` command before this skill was invoked. The classification header has already been emitted. Do NOT emit it again. This skill handles **execution only** for STANDARD and STRICT tier tasks.\n>\n> If for any reason no classification header was emitted before this skill was invoked, emit one now using ONLY the tier values STRICT, STANDARD, LIGHT, or EXEMPT (no other values are valid).\n\n# /sc:task-unified - Unified Task Execution with Compliance\n\n## Purpose\n\nUnified command that merges orchestration capabilities with MCP compliance enforcement. Automatically classifies tasks into compliance tiers and enforces appropriate verification.\n\n**Key Benefits**:\n- Single command replaces confusion between `sc:task` and `sc:task-mcp`\n- Automatic tier classification with confidence scoring\n- Appropriate verification for each task type\n- Prevents over-engineering for trivial changes\n- Ensures safety for critical changes\n\n## Triggers\n\nUse `/sc:task` when:\n- Task involves code modifications with downstream impacts\n- Complexity score >0.6 with code modifications\n- Multi-file scope (>2 estimated affected files)\n- Security domain paths detected (auth/, security/, crypto/)\n- Refactoring or system-wide changes requested\n\n**Auto-Suggest Keywords**:\n- High confidence: \"implement feature\", \"refactor system\", \"fix security\", \"add authentication\", \"update database schema\"\n- Moderate confidence: \"add new\", \"create component\", \"update service\", \"modify API\"\n\n## Usage\n\n```bash\n/sc:task [description]                           # Auto-detect all dimensions\n/sc:task [description] --compliance strict       # Force STRICT tier\n/sc:task [description] --compliance light        # Force LIGHT tier\n/sc:task [description] --skip-compliance         # Bypass compliance (escape hatch)\n/sc:task [description] --verify auto             # Auto-select verification\n```\n\n## Behavioral Flow\n\n### 0. Classification (Already Performed)\n\nClassification was handled by the `/sc:task` command before this skill was invoked. The tier has been determined and the classification header has been emitted. Proceed directly to execution based on the classified tier.\n\n**Reference** — the tier keyword tables (for context only, do not re-classify):\n- **STRICT**: security, authentication, database, migration, refactor, breaking change, encrypt, token, session, oauth\n- **EXEMPT**: explain, search, commit, push, plan, discuss, brainstorm\n- **LIGHT**: typo, comment, whitespace, lint, docstring, formatting, minor\n- **STANDARD**: implement, add, create, update, fix, build, modify, change (default)\n\n### 2. Confidence Display (Human-Readable)\n\nAfter the mandatory classification header, show a human-readable summary:\n\n```\n**Tier: STANDARD** [████████░░] 80%\n\nClassified as STANDARD:\n- Keywords matched: add, implement\n- Confidence score: 0.78\n- Considered alternatives: STRICT (0.35)\n```\n\nIf confidence <70%, add prompt: \"⚠️ Low confidence. Override with: `--compliance [strict|standard|light|exempt]`\"\n\n**Note**: The machine-readable header (Section 0) is for telemetry/A/B testing. This human-readable display is for user understanding.\n\n### 3. Execution Phase\n\nExecute task according to tier requirements:\n\n**STRICT Execution**:\n1. Activate project (mcp__serena__activate_project)\n2. Verify git working directory clean (git status)\n3. Load codebase context (codebase-retrieval)\n4. Check relevant memories (list_memories -> read_memory)\n5. Identify all affected files and test files\n6. Make changes with full checklist\n7. Identify all files that import changed code\n8. Update all affected files\n9. Spawn verification agent (quality-engineer)\n10. Run comprehensive tests: `pytest [path] -v`\n11. Answer adversarial questions\n\n**STANDARD Execution**:\n1. Load context via codebase-retrieval\n2. Search downstream impacts (find_referencing_symbols OR grep)\n3. Make changes\n4. Run affected tests OR document manual verification\n5. Verify basic functionality\n\n**LIGHT Execution**:\n1. Quick scope check (files/lines within bounds)\n2. Make changes\n3. Quick sanity check (syntax valid, no obvious errors)\n4. Proceed with judgment\n\n**EXEMPT Execution**:\n1. Execute immediately\n2. No verification overhead\n\n### 4. Verification Phase\n\nRoute to appropriate verification based on tier and paths:\n\n| Compliance Tier | Verification Method | Token Cost | Timeout |\n|-----------------|---------------------|------------|---------|\n| STRICT | Sub-agent (quality-engineer) | 3-5K | 60s |\n| STANDARD | Direct test execution | 300-500 | 30s |\n| LIGHT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n| EXEMPT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n\n**Critical Path Override**: Paths matching `auth/`, `security/`, `crypto/`, `models/`, `migrations/` always trigger CRITICAL verification regardless of compliance tier.\n\n**Trivial Path Override**: Paths matching `*.md`, `docs/`, `*test*.py` may skip verification.\n\n### 5. Feedback Collection\n\nAfter completion, collect implicit feedback:\n- Track if user overrode tier (implicit classification feedback)\n- Note smooth completion vs errors (quality signal)\n- Store for calibration learning\n\n## MCP Integration\n\n**Required Servers by Tier**:\n- STRICT: Sequential, Serena (fallback not allowed)\n- STANDARD: Sequential, Context7 (fallback allowed)\n- LIGHT: None required (fallback allowed)\n- EXEMPT: None required\n\n**Circuit Breaker Behavior**:\n- If required servers unavailable for STRICT tier, block task execution\n- For other tiers, use fallbacks with noted limitations\n\n## Tool Coordination\n\n**Planning Phase**:\n1. TodoWrite: Create task breakdown\n2. codebase-retrieval: Load context\n3. list_memories / read_memory: Check project state\n\n**Execution Phase**:\n1. Edit/MultiEdit/Write: Make changes\n2. Grep/Glob: Find references\n3. find_referencing_symbols: Trace dependencies\n\n**Verification Phase**:\n1. Task (quality-engineer): Spawn verification agent (STRICT only)\n2. Bash: Run tests directly (STANDARD)\n3. think_about_task_adherence: Reflect on completeness\n\n**Completion Phase**:\n1. write_memory: Save session state\n2. think_about_whether_you_are_done: Final check\n\n## Examples\n\n### STRICT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"implement user authentication with JWT\"\n\n-> Classified as STRICT (security domain, authentication keyword)\n-> Full 6-category checklist enforced\n-> Verification agent spawned\n-> Adversarial questions answered\n```\n\n### STANDARD Task\n```\n/sc:task \"add pagination to user list\"\n\n-> Classified as STANDARD (add keyword, typical feature)\n-> Context loaded before editing\n-> Downstream impacts checked\n-> Direct test execution\n```\n\n### LIGHT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"fix typo in README\"\n\n-> Classified as LIGHT (trivial keyword, documentation path)\n-> Quick sanity check only\n-> No verification delay\n```\n\n### EXEMPT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"explain how the auth flow works\"\n\n-> Classified as EXEMPT (explain pattern detected)\n-> Immediate execution\n-> No compliance overhead\n```\n\n### Override Example\n```\n/sc:task \"update config file\" --compliance strict\n\n-> User override to STRICT regardless of classification\n-> Full verification enforced\n```\n\n## Boundaries\n\n**Will:**\n- Classify tasks into appropriate compliance tiers\n- Enforce tier-appropriate verification requirements\n- Provide confidence scoring with rationale\n- Track feedback for continuous calibration\n- Support user overrides with justification\n\n**Will Not:**\n- Skip safety-critical verification for STRICT tasks\n- Apply STRICT overhead to genuinely trivial changes\n- Override user's explicit compliance choice\n- Proceed with <70% confidence without user confirmation\n\n## Success Criteria\n\n| Metric | Target | Measurement |\n|--------|--------|-------------|\n| Tier classification accuracy | >=80% | User feedback on appropriateness |\n| User confusion rate | <10% | \"Which command?\" questions eliminated |\n| Skip rate (--skip-compliance) | <12% | Override tracking |\n| Regression prevention | >=85% | Post-verification bug detection |\n| STRICT tier overhead | <25% | Execution telemetry |\n\n## Configuration References\n\n- Keywords: `config/tier-keywords.yaml`\n- Verification routing: `config/verification-routing.yaml`\n- Acceptance criteria: `config/tier-acceptance-criteria.yaml`\n- Circuit breakers: See MCP.md\n- Routing logic: See ORCHESTRATOR.md\n\n\nARGUMENTS: Execute all tasks in /config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.0_unified-audit-gating/phase-5-tasklist.md --compliance strict --strategy \"## HALT — Sprint Paused\",\n   555→        \"\",\n   556→        \"### Resume Command\",\n   557→        f\"```\",\n   558→        f\"superclaude sprint run {config.index_path} --resume Extracts:\n    29→    - imports: All import statements (import X, from X import Y)\n    30→    - exports: Symbols listed in __all__, or top-level public definitions\n    31→    - references: Cross-file name references (function calls, attribute access)\n    32→\n    33→    Handles SyntaxError gracefully per R2: returns empty FileAnalysis with\n    34→    error metadata instead of raising.\n    35→\n    36→    Args:\n    37→        file_path: Path to the file being analyzed.\n    38→        content: Source code content of the file.\n    39→\n    40→    Returns:\n    41→        FileAnalysis with populated imports, exports, and references.\n    42→    \"\"\"\n    43→    content_hash = compute_content_hash(content)\n    44→    lines = content.splitlines()\n    45→\n    46→ },\n    77→    )\n    78→\n    79→\n    80→def _extract_imports(tree: ast.Module) -> list[str]:\n    81→    \"\"\"Extract import statements from AST.\"\"\"\n    82→    imports: list[str] = []\n    83→\n    84→    for node in ast.walk(tree):\n    85→        if isinstance(node, ast.Import):\n    86→            for alias in node.names:\n    87→                imports.append(f\"import {alias.name}\")\n    88→        elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):\n    89→            module = node.module or \"\"\n    90→            for alias in node.names:\n    91→                imports.append(f\"from {module} import {alias.name}\")\n    92→\n    93→    return imports\n    94→\n    95→\n    96→def _extract_exports(tree: ast.Module) -> list[str]:\n    97→    \"\"\"Extract exported symbols from AST.\n    98→\n    99→    Looks for __all__ assignment first. If absent, collects top-level\n   100→    public function and class definitions.\n   101→    \"\"\"\n   102→    # Check for __all__\n   103→    for node in ast.iter_child_nodes(tree):\n   104→        if isinstance(node, ast.Assign):\n   105→            for target in node.targets:\n   106→                if isinstance(target, ast.Name) and target.id == \"__all__\":\n   107→                    if isinstance(node.value, (ast.List, ast.Tuple)):\n   108→                        return [\n   109→                            elt.value\n   110→                            for elt in node.value.elts\n   111→                            if isinstance(elt, ast.Constant) and isinstance(elt.value, str)\n   112→                        ]\n   113→\n   114→    # Fallback: top-level public definitions\n   115→    exports: list[str] = []\n   116→    for node in ast.iter_child_nodes(tree):\n   117→        if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef)):\n   118→            if not node.name.startswith(\"_\"):\n   119→                exports.append(node.name)\n   120→        elif isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef):\n   121→            if not node.name.startswith(\"_\"):\n   122→                exports.append(node.name)\n   123→\n   124→    return exports\n   125→\n   126→\n   127→def _extract_references(tree: ast.Module) -> list[str]:\n   128→    \"\"\"Extract cross-file name references from AST.\n   129→\n   130→    Collects:\n   131→    - | None = None,\n   295→    budget_remaining: int = 0,\n   296→) -> AggregatedPhaseReport:\n   297→    \"\"\"Aggregate individual TaskResults into a runner-constructed PhaseReport.\n   298→\n   299→    This function is the runner's authoritative source of task outcomes.\n   300→    It does not rely on agent self-reporting.\n   301→\n   302→    Args:\n   303→        phase_number: The phase number being aggregated.\n   304→        task_results: List of TaskResult from execute_phase_tasks().\n   305→        remaining_task_ids: Task IDs that were not attempted due to budget.\n   306→\n   307→    Returns:\n   308→        AggregatedPhaseReport with computed counts and status.\n   309→    \"\"\"\n   310→    report = AggregatedPhaseReport(\n   311→        phase_number=phase_number,\n   312→ len(task_results) + len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n   318→    report.tasks_passed = sum(1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS)\n   319→    report.tasks_failed = sum(1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.FAIL)\n   320→    report.tasks_incomplete = sum(\n   321→        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n   322→    )\n   323→    report.tasks_skipped = sum(\n   324→        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED\n   325→    )\n   326→    report.tasks_not_attempted = len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n   327→    report.total_turns_consumed = sum(r.turns_consumed for r in task_results)\n   328→    report.total_duration_seconds = sum(r.duration_seconds for r in task_results)\n   329→\n   330→    return report\n   331→\n   332→\n   333→def check_budget_guard(ledger: TurnLedger | None) -> str | None:\n   334→    \"\"\"Pre-launch budget guard: returns a halt message if budget is insufficient.\n   335→\n   336→    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n   337→    if the budget is too low to launch a subprocess.\n   338→    \"\"\"\n   339→    if ledger is None:\n   340→        return None\n   341→    if ledger.can_launch():\n   342→        return None\n   343→    return (\n   344→        f\"Budget exhausted: {ledger.available()} turns remaining, \"\n   345→        f\"minimum {ledger.minimum_allocation} required for launch\"\n   346→    )\n   347→\n   348→\n   349→def execute_phase_tasks(\n   350→    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n   351→    config: SprintConfig,\n   352→    phase,\n   353→    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n   354→    *,\n   355→    _subprocess_factory=None,\n   356→) -> tuple[list[TaskResult], list[str]]:\n   357→    \"\"\"Per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\n   358→\n   359→    Iterates over a task inventory, spawning one subprocess per task with\n   360→    budget allocation from the TurnLedger. Returns task results and a list\n   361→    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n   362→\n   363→    Args:\n   364→        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n   365→        config: Sprint configuration.\n   366→        phase: The Phase being executed.\n   367→        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n   368→        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n   369→            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n   370→\n   371→    Returns:\n   372→        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n   373→        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n   374→    \"\"\"\n   375→    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n   376→    remaining: list[str] = []\n   377→\n   378→    if not tasks:\n   379→        return results, remaining\n   380→\n   381→    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n   382→        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   383→\n   384→        # Budget check: can we launch?\n   385→        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\n   386→            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\n   387→            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\n   388→            for t in tasks[i:]:\n   389→                results.append(\n   390→                    TaskResult(\n   391→                        task=t,\n   392→                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\n   393→                        started_at=started_at,\n   394→                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   395→                    )\n   396→                )\n   397→            break\n   398→\n   399→        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\n   400→        if ledger is not None:\n   401→            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\n   402→\n   403→        # Spawn subprocess for this task\n   404→        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\n   405→            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\n   406→                task, config, phase\n   407→            )\n   408→        else:\n   409→            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\n   410→            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\n   411→                task, config, phase\n   412→            )\n   413→\n   414→        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   415→\n   416→        # Determine task status from exit code\n   417→        if exit_code == 0:\n   418→            status = TaskStatus.PASS\n   419→        elif exit_code == 124:\n   420→            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n   421→        else:\n   422→            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n   423→\n   424→        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\n   425→        if ledger is not None:\n   426→            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\n   427→            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\n   428→            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\n   429→            if actual > pre_allocated:\n   430→                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\n   431→            elif actual < pre_allocated:\n   432→                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\n   433→\n   434→        results.append(\n   435→            TaskResult(\n   436→                task=task,\n   437→                status=status,\n   438→                turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\n   439→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   440→                started_at=started_at,\n   441→                finished_at=finished_at,\n   442→                output_bytes=output_bytes,\n   443→            )\n   444→        )\n   445→\n   446→    return results, remaining\n   447→\n   448→\n   449→def _run_task_subprocess(\n   450→    task: TaskEntry,\n   451→    config: SprintConfig,\n   452→    phase,\n   453→) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n   454→    \"\"\"Run a single task in a subprocess. Returns (exit_code, turns, output_bytes).\n   455→\n   456→    This is the real implementation that spawns a ClaudeProcess. For testing,\n   457→    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n   458→    \"\"\"\n   459→    # Build a task-specific prompt\n   460→    prompt = (\n   461→        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n   462→        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n   463→        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n   464→    )\n   465→\n   466→    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n   467→    proc.config = config\n   468→    proc.phase = phase\n   469→    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n   470→\n   471→    _Base.__init__(\n   472→        proc,\n   473→        prompt=prompt,\n   474→        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n   475→        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n   476→        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n   477→        model=config.model,\n   478→        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n   479→        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n   480→        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n   481→    )\n   482→    proc.start()\n   483→    proc.wait()\n   484→    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n   485→    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n   486→    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n   487→    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n   488→    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)\n   489→\n   490→\n   491→def execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n   492→    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n   493→\n   494→    For each active phase:\n   495→    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n   496→    2. Start output monitor thread\n   497→    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n   498→    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n   499→    5. Record PhaseResult\n   500→    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n   501→    \"\"\"\n   502→    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n   503→    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n   504→        raise SystemExit(\n   505→            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n   506→            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n   507→        )\n   508→\n   509→    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n   510→    signal_handler.install()\n   511→\n   512→    setup_debug_logger(config)\n   513→    import logging as _logging\n   514→\n   515→    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n   516→\n   517→    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n   518→    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n   519→    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n   520→    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n   521→\n   522→    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n   523→    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n   524→\n   525→    tui.start()\n   526→\n   527→    # Startup orphan cleanup: remove stale isolation dirs from crashed previous runs\n   528→    shutil.rmtree(config.results_dir / \".isolation\", ignore_errors=True)\n   529→\n   530→    # Execute all python-mode phases via preflight executor before the main loop.\n   531→    # Removing this single call reverts to all-Claude behavior (R-051 rollback property).\n   532→    # Lazy import breaks the preflight → executor → preflight circular import cycle.\n   533→    from .preflight import execute_preflight_phases  # noqa: PLC0415\n   534→\n   535→    preflight_results = execute_preflight_phases(config)\n   536→\n   537→    try:\n   538→        for phase in config.active_phases:\n   539→            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   540→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n   541→                break\n   542→\n   543→            # Python-mode phases were already executed by preflight; skip here.\n   544→            if phase.execution_mode == \"python\":\n   545→                continue\n   546→\n   547→            # Skip-mode phases: record SKIPPED with no subprocess launched.\n   548→            if phase.execution_mode == \"skip\":\n   549→                _now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   550→                skip_result = PhaseResult(\n   551→                    phase=phase,\n   552→                    status=PhaseStatus.SKIPPED,\n   553→                    exit_code=0,\n   554→                    started_at=_now,\n   555→                    finished_at=_now,\n   556→                )\n   557→                sprint_result.phase_results.append(skip_result)\n   558→                logger.write_phase_result(skip_result)\n   559→                continue\n   560→\n   561→            # Per-phase isolation directory: exactly one file (the phase file)\n   562→            isolation_dir = config.results_dir / \".isolation\" / f\"phase-{phase.number}\"\n   563→            isolation_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   564→            shutil.copy2(phase.file, isolation_dir / phase.file.name)\n   565→\n   566→            try:\n   567→                # Reset monitor for this phase\n   568→                output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n   569→                monitor.reset(output_path)\n   570→                monitor.start()\n   571→\n   572→                # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n   573→                if config.tmux_session_name:\n   574→                    update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n   575→\n   576→                # Launch claude with isolation env vars (CLAUDE_WORK_DIR → isolation_dir)\n   577→                _phase_env_vars = {\n   578→                    \"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\": str(isolation_dir),\n   579→                }\n   580→                proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase, env_vars=_phase_env_vars)\n   581→                proc_manager.start()\n   582→                started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   583→                # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n   584→                deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n   585→                logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n   586→\n   587→                debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n   588→\n   589→                tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n   590→\n   591→                # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n   592→                # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n   593→                _timed_out = False\n   594→                _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n   595→                _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n   596→                while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n   597→                    if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   598→                        proc_manager.terminate()\n   599→                        break\n   600→                    if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n   601→                        # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n   602→                        _timed_out = True\n   603→                        proc_manager.terminate()\n   604→                        break\n   605→\n   606→                    ms = monitor.state\n   607→                    _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n   608→\n   609→                    debug_log(\n   610→                        _dbg,\n   611→                        \"poll_tick\",\n   612→                        phase=phase.number,\n   613→                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n   614→                        poll_result=\"running\",\n   615→                        elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n   616→                        output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n   617→                        growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n   618→                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n   619→                        stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n   620→                    )\n   621→\n   622→                    # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n   623→                    if (\n   624→                        config.stall_timeout > 0\n   625→                        and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n   626→                        and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n   627→                        and not _stall_acted\n   628→                    ):\n   629→                        _stall_acted = True\n   630→                        debug_log(\n   631→                            _dbg,\n   632→                            \"watchdog_triggered\",\n   633→                            phase=phase.number,\n   634→                            action=config.stall_action,\n   635→                            stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n   636→                            pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n   637→                        )\n   638→                        if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n   639→                            import sys\n   640→\n   641→                            print(\n   642→                                f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n   643→                                f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n   644→                                file=sys.stderr,\n   645→                            )\n   646→                            _timed_out = True\n   647→                            proc_manager.terminate()\n   648→                            break\n   649→                        else:\n   650→                            # warn action: log and continue\n   651→                            import sys\n   652→\n   653→                            print(\n   654→                                f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n   655→                                f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n   656→                                file=sys.stderr,\n   657→                            )\n   658→\n   659→                    # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n   660→                    if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n   661→                        _stall_acted = False\n   662→\n   663→                    # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n   664→                    # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n   665→                    try:\n   666→                        tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n   667→                    except Exception as _tui_exc:\n   668→                        import sys\n   669→\n   670→                        print(\n   671→                            f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\",\n   672→                            file=sys.stderr,\n   673→                        )\n   674→                    time.sleep(0.5)\n   675→\n   676→                # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n   677→                # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n   678→                raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n   679→                if _timed_out:\n   680→                    exit_code = 124\n   681→                else:\n   682→                    exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n   683→                monitor.stop()\n   684→                finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   685→                _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n   686→                debug_log(\n   687→                    _dbg,\n   688→                    \"PHASE_END\",\n   689→                    phase=phase.number,\n   690→                    exit_code=exit_code,\n   691→                    duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n   692→                )\n   693→\n   694→                # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n   695→                # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n   696→                # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n   697→                # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n   698→                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   699→                    logger.write_phase_interrupt(\n   700→                        phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code\n   701→                    )\n   702→                    sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n   703→                    break\n   704→\n   705→                # Write a preliminary result sentinel so _determine_phase_status()\n   706→                # always finds a result file for exit_code=0 phases that wrote no report.\n   707→                # Guard: only for successful exits; non-zero paths must not reach this.\n   708→                if exit_code == 0:\n   709→                    _wrote_preliminary = _write_preliminary_result(\n   710→                        config, phase, started_at.timestamp()\n   711→                    )\n   712→                    debug_log(\n   713→                        _dbg,\n   714→                        \"preliminary_result_write\",\n   715→                        path=f\"{'executor-preliminary (option_d)' if _wrote_preliminary else 'agent-written/option_a_or_noop'}\",\n   716→                    )\n   717→\n   718→                # Determine phase status\n   719→                status = _determine_phase_status(\n   720→                    exit_code=exit_code,\n   721→                    result_file=config.result_file(phase),\n   722→                    output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n   723→                    config=config,\n   724→                    phase=phase,\n   725→                    started_at=started_at.timestamp(),\n   726→                    error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n   727→                )\n   728→\n   729→                # Write executor result file for downstream consumers.\n   730→                # Written AFTER status determination to avoid circularity.\n   731→                # Overwrites any agent-written file — executor is authoritative.\n   732→                _write_executor_result_file(\n   733→                    config=config,\n   734→                    phase=phase,\n   735→                    status=status,\n   736→                    exit_code=exit_code,\n   737→                    monitor_state=monitor.state,\n   738→                    started_at=started_at,\n   739→                    finished_at=finished_at,\n   740→                )\n   741→\n   742→                # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n   743→                error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n   744→                error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n   745→\n   746→                phase_result = PhaseResult(\n   747→                    phase=phase,\n   748→                    status=status,\n   749→                    exit_code=exit_code,\n   750→                    started_at=started_at,\n   751→                    finished_at=finished_at,\n   752→                    output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n   753→                    error_bytes=error_bytes,\n   754→                    last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n   755→                    files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n   756→                )\n   757→                sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n   758→\n   759→                debug_log(\n   760→                    _dbg,\n   761→                    \"phase_complete\",\n   762→                    phase=phase.number,\n   763→                    status=status.value,\n   764→                    exit_code=exit_code,\n   765→                    duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n   766→                )\n   767→\n   768→                # Log and notify\n   769→                logger.write_phase_result(phase_result)\n   770→                notify_phase_complete(phase_result)\n   771→\n   772→                tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, None)\n   773→\n   774→                # Decide: continue or halt?\n   775→                if status.is_failure:\n   776→                    # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n   777→                    try:\n   778→                        collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n   779→                        bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n   780→                        classifier = FailureClassifier()\n   781→                        bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n   782→                        reporter = ReportGenerator()\n   783→                        diag_path = (\n   784→                            config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n   785→                        )\n   786→                        reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n   787→                        debug_log(\n   788→                            _dbg,\n   789→                            \"diagnostic_report\",\n   790→                            phase=phase.number,\n   791→                            category=bundle.category.value,\n   792→                            path=str(diag_path),\n   793→                        )\n   794→                    except Exception as _diag_exc:\n   795→                        debug_log(\n   796→                            _dbg,\n   797→                            \"diagnostic_error\",\n   798→                            phase=phase.number,\n   799→                            error=str(_diag_exc),\n   800→                        )\n   801→\n   802→                    sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n   803→                    sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n   804→                    break\n   805→\n   806→            finally:\n   807→                shutil.rmtree(isolation_dir, ignore_errors=True)\n   808→\n   809→        # Merge preflight results with main-loop results in original phase order.\n   810→        # Build a lookup from phase number → PhaseResult, main-loop results win\n   811→        # on conflict (they are the authoritative executor record for claude phases).\n   812→        _merged: dict[int, PhaseResult] = {r.phase.number: r for r in preflight_results}\n   813→        for r in sprint_result.phase_results:\n   814→            _merged[r.phase.number] = r\n   815→        sprint_result.phase_results = [\n   816→            _merged[p.number] for p in config.active_phases if p.number in _merged\n   817→        ]\n   818→\n   819→        # Sprint finished\n   820→        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   821→        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n   822→            # Verify all phases actually passed\n   823→            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n   824→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n   825→\n   826→        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n   827→        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n   828→        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n   829→\n   830→    finally:\n   831→        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n   832→        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n   833→        try:\n   834→            monitor.stop()\n   835→        except Exception:\n   836→            pass\n   837→        if proc_manager is not None:\n   838→            try:\n   839→                proc_manager.terminate()\n   840→            except Exception:\n   841→                pass\n   842→        try:\n   843→            tui.stop()\n   844→        except Exception:\n   845→            pass\n   846→        try:\n   847→            signal_handler.uninstall()\n   848→        except Exception:\n   849→            pass\n   850→\n   851→    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n   852→    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n   853→    try:\n   854→        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n   855→    except OSError:\n   856→        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n   857→\n   858→    if _exitcode != 0:\n   859→        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n   860→\n   861→\n   862→def _classify_from_result_file(\n   863→    result_file: Path,\n   864→    started_at: float,\n   865→) -> PhaseStatus | None:\n   866→    \"\"\"Classify phase outcome from the agent-written result file.\n   867→\n   868→    Returns a PhaseStatus if the result file exists, is fresh (mtime > started_at),\n   869→    and contains a recognizable EXIT_RECOMMENDATION. Returns None if the file is\n   870→    missing, stale, or unreadable.\n   871→    \"\"\"\n   872→    if not result_file.exists():\n   873→        return None\n   874→    try:\n   875→        mtime = result_file.stat().st_mtime\n   876→    except OSError:\n   877→        return None\n   878→    if started_at > 0 and mtime < started_at:\n   879→        # Stale file from a previous run — do not trust\n   880→        return None\n   881→    try:\n   882→        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n   883→    except OSError:\n   884→        return None\n   885→    upper = content.upper()\n   886→    if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper:\n   887→        return PhaseStatus.HALT\n   888→    if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper:\n   889→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_RECOVERED\n   890→    if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   891→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_RECOVERED\n   892→    if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   893→        return PhaseStatus.HALT\n   894→    if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   895→        return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n   896→    return None\n   897→\n   898→\n   899→def _check_checkpoint_pass(config: SprintConfig, phase: Phase) -> bool:\n   900→    \"\"\"Return True if the end-of-phase checkpoint file exists with status PASS.\"\"\"\n   901→    checkpoint_path = (\n   902→        config.release_dir / \"checkpoints\" / f\"CP-P{phase.number:02d}-END.md\"\n   903→    )\n   904→    if not checkpoint_path.exists():\n   905→        return False\n   906→    try:\n   907→        content = checkpoint_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\").upper()\n   908→        return \"STATUS: PASS\" in content or \"**RESULT**: PASS\" in content\n   909→    except OSError:\n   910→        return False\n   911→\n   912→\n   913→def _check_contamination(config: SprintConfig, phase: Phase) -> list[str]:\n   914→    \"\"\"Return list of artifact files containing cross-phase task ID patterns.\"\"\"\n   915→    import re as _re\n   916→\n   917→    contaminated: list[str] = []\n   918→    artifacts_dir = config.release_dir / \"artifacts\"\n   919→    if not artifacts_dir.exists():\n   920→        return contaminated\n   921→    next_phase = phase.number + 1\n   922→    pattern = _re.compile(rf\"T{next_phase:02d}\\.\\d{{2}}\", _re.IGNORECASE)\n   923→    for md_file in artifacts_dir.rglob(\"*.md\"):\n   924→        try:\n   925→            if pattern.search(md_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")):\n   926→                contaminated.append(str(md_file.relative_to(config.release_dir)))\n   927→        except OSError:\n   928→            pass\n   929→    return contaminated\n   930→\n   931→\n   932→def _write_crash_recovery_log(\n   933→    config: SprintConfig,\n   934→    phase: Phase,\n   935→    contaminated: list[str],\n   936→) -> None:\n   937→    \"\"\"Append crash recovery entry to results/crash_recovery_log.md.\"\"\"\n   938→    log_path = config.results_dir / \"crash_recovery_log.md\"\n   939→    entry = (\n   940→        f\"\\n## Phase {phase.number} — PASS_RECOVERED Recovery\\n\"\n   941→        f\"**Timestamp**: {datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()}\\n\"\n   942→        f\"**Checkpoint**: checkpoints/CP-P{phase.number:02d}-END.md (PASS)\\n\"\n   943→        f\"**Contamination check**: \"\n   944→        + (\n   945→            \"CLEAN\"\n   946→            if not contaminated\n   947→            else f\"WARNING — {len(contaminated)} file(s): {contaminated}\"\n   948→        )\n   949→        + \"\\n\"\n   950→        \"**Action**: Phase reclassified ERROR→PASS_RECOVERED.\\n\"\n   951→    )\n   952→    try:\n   953→        with open(log_path, \"a\") as f:\n   954→            f.write(entry)\n   955→    except OSError:\n   956→        pass\n   957→\n   958→\n   959→def _write_preliminary_result(\n   960→    config: SprintConfig,\n   961→    phase: Phase,\n   962→    started_at: float,\n   963→) -> bool:\n   964→    \"\"\"Write a preliminary result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE sentinel.\n   965→\n   966→    This function is a deterministic fallback ensuring ``_determine_phase_status()``\n   967→    always finds a valid result file for phases that exit 0 but write no report.\n   968→    Without it, such phases return ``PASS_NO_REPORT`` because no result file is present.\n   969→\n   970→    **Ordering invariant**: Call *after* ``finished_at`` is captured and *before*\n   971→    ``_determine_phase_status()`` is called. If called after status determination,\n   972→    the sentinel file will not affect the already-computed status.\n   973→\n   974→    **Concurrency**: Assumes single-threaded execution (one phase at a time). If the\n   975→    sprint loop is parallelised in future, this function must be replaced with an\n   976→    ``O_EXCL``-based atomic write to prevent TOCTOU races between the exists-check\n   977→    and the write.\n   978→\n   979→    **Sentinel contract**: Writes ``EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\\\n`` so that\n   980→    ``_determine_phase_status()`` branch 3 (``result_file.exists()`` → CONTINUE check\n   981→    at line 1024) returns ``PhaseStatus.PASS`` instead of falling through to the\n   982→    ``PASS_NO_REPORT`` branch (line 1045). This sentinel is intentionally minimal;\n   983→    the executor result file written by ``_write_executor_result_file()`` afterwards\n   984→    will overwrite it with the authoritative structured report.\n   985→\n   986→    Args:\n   987→        config: Sprint configuration providing ``result_file(phase)`` path.\n   988→        phase: The phase whose result file to write.\n   989→        started_at: Phase start time as a POSIX timestamp (``datetime.timestamp()``).\n   990→            Used for the freshness guard: if a result file already exists with\n   991→            ``st_mtime >= started_at`` and ``st_size > 0``, it is assumed to be a\n   992→            valid agent-written file and is left untouched (no-op).\n   993→\n   994→    Returns:\n   995→        ``True`` if the sentinel file was written, ``False`` if the file was preserved\n   996→        (freshness guard triggered) or an ``OSError`` prevented the write. The boolean\n   997→        is intended for telemetry logging only; callers should not branch on it.\n   998→    \"\"\"\n   999→    import logging as _logging\n  1000→\n  1001→    result_path = config.result_file(phase)\n  1002→    # Freshness guard: if a valid, fresh agent-written file is present, do not overwrite.\n  1003→    if result_path.exists():\n  1004→        try:\n  1005→            st = result_path.stat()\n  1006→            if st.st_size > 0 and st.st_mtime >= started_at:\n  1007→                # Fresh, non-empty agent file — preserve it\n  1008→                return False\n  1009→        except OSError:\n  1010→            pass  # Treat stat failure as absent; fall through to write\n  1011→\n  1012→    # Write the sentinel: zero-byte, stale, or missing files are all overwritten.\n  1013→    try:\n  1014→        result_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n  1015→        result_path.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n  1016→        return True\n  1017→    except OSError as exc:\n  1018→        _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(\n  1019→            \"preliminary result write failed: %s; phase may report PASS_NO_REPORT\",\n  1020→            exc,\n  1021→        )\n  1022→        return False\n  1023→\n  1024→\n  1025→def _write_executor_result_file(\n  1026→    config: SprintConfig,\n  1027→    phase: Phase,\n  1028→    status: PhaseStatus,\n  1029→    exit_code: int,\n  1030→    monitor_state: MonitorState,\n  1031→    started_at: datetime,\n  1032→    finished_at: datetime,\n  1033→) -> None:\n  1034→    \"\"\"Write executor-sourced result file for downstream consumers.\n  1035→\n  1036→    This is written AFTER _determine_phase_status returns, so it does not\n  1037→    create circularity. It provides a deterministic result file even when\n  1038→    the agent failed to write one.\n  1039→    \"\"\"\n  1040→    duration = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n  1041→    recommendation = \"CONTINUE\" if status.is_success else \"HALT\"\n  1042→    content = (\n  1043→        \"---\\n\"\n  1044→        f\"phase: {phase.number}\\n\"\n  1045→        f\"status: {'PASS' if status.is_success else 'FAIL'}\\n\"\n  1046→        f\"tasks_total: 1\\n\"\n  1047→        f\"tasks_passed: {1 if status.is_success else 0}\\n\"\n  1048→        f\"tasks_failed: {0 if status.is_success else 1}\\n\"\n  1049→        \"---\\n\"\n  1050→        \"\\n\"\n  1051→        f\"# Phase {phase.number} — Executor Result Report\\n\"\n  1052→        \"\\n\"\n  1053→        f\"| Phase | Status | Exit Code | Duration |\\n\"\n  1054→        f\"|-------|--------|-----------|----------|\\n\"\n  1055→        f\"| {phase.number} | {status.value} | {exit_code} | {duration:.1f}s |\\n\"\n  1056→        \"\\n\"\n  1057→        f\"**Source**: executor (not agent self-report)\\n\"\n  1058→        f\"**Output bytes**: {monitor_state.output_bytes}\\n\"\n  1059→        f\"**Last task ID**: {monitor_state.last_task_id or 'n/a'}\\n\"\n  1060→        f\"**Files changed**: {monitor_state.files_changed}\\n\"\n  1061→        \"\\n\"\n  1062→        f\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: {recommendation}\\n\"\n  1063→    )\n  1064→    result_path = config.result_file(phase)\n  1065→    try:\n  1066→        result_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n  1067→        result_path.write_text(content)\n  1068→    except OSError:\n  1069→        pass  # Non-fatal — best effort\n  1070→\n  1071→\n  1072→def _determine_phase_status(\n  1073→    exit_code: int,\n  1074→    result_file: Path,\n  1075→    output_file: Path,\n  1076→    *,\n  1077→    config: SprintConfig | None = None,\n  1078→    phase: Phase | None = None,\n  1079→    started_at: float = 0.0,\n  1080→    error_file: Path | None = None,\n  1081→) -> PhaseStatus:\n  1082→    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n  1083→\n  1084→    Priority:\n  1085→    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n  1086→    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n  1087→    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n  1088→    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n  1089→    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n  1090→    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n  1091→    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n  1092→    \"\"\"\n  1093→    if exit_code == 124:\n  1094→        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n  1095→    if exit_code != 0:\n  1096→        # Path 1 — Specific: context exhaustion (Spec B S2)\n  1097→        # detect_prompt_too_long reads NDJSON output for \"Prompt is too long\"\n  1098→        if detect_prompt_too_long(output_file, error_path=error_file):\n  1099→            # Check if the agent managed to write a result file before exhaustion\n  1100→            result_status = _classify_from_result_file(result_file, started_at)\n  1101→            if result_status is not None:\n  1102→                return result_status\n  1103→            # No valid result file — context exhausted without completing\n  1104→            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n  1105→\n  1106→        # Path 2 — General: checkpoint inference (Spec A SOL-C)\n  1107→        # Reads agent-written checkpoint files (pre-crash evidence)\n  1108→        if config is not None and phase is not None:\n  1109→            if _check_checkpoint_pass(config, phase):\n  1110→                contaminated = _check_contamination(config, phase)\n  1111→                _write_crash_recovery_log(config, phase, contaminated)\n  1112→                if not contaminated:\n  1113→                    return PhaseStatus.PASS_RECOVERED\n  1114→\n  1115→        # Path 3 — Default: unchanged\n  1116→        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n  1117→\n  1118→    if result_file.exists():\n  1119→        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n  1120→        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n  1121→        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n  1122→        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n  1123→        upper = content.upper()\n  1124→        # Sentinel contract: EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE is written by\n  1125→        # _write_preliminary_result() as a deterministic fallback for phases that\n  1126→        # exit 0 but produce no agent result file. The presence of this sentinel\n  1127→        # here causes the phase to return PASS rather than fall through to\n  1128→        # PASS_NO_REPORT (branch 4). _write_executor_result_file() overwrites this\n  1129→        # file after status determination with the authoritative structured report.\n  1130→        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n  1131→        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n  1132→        if has_halt:\n  1133→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n  1134→        if has_continue:\n  1135→            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n  1136→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n  1137→            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n  1138→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n  1139→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n  1140→        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n  1141→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n  1142→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n  1143→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n  1144→\n  1145→    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n  1146→        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n  1147→        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n  1148→        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n  1149→        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n  1150→            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n  1151→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n  1152→\n  1153→    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n  1154→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01BFD5iryeKmRo68vY6n3RkB","session_id":"f20d372f-daed-4f98-960f-767cfbba599c","uuid":"de2d379b-d6e8-4425-92f6-cc2ea29028d4","timestamp":"2026-03-19T05:08:54.274Z"}

194→\n   195→\n   196→def _extract_injectable_params(\n   197→    tree: ast.Module, file_path: str\n   198→) -> list[tuple[str, str, int]]:\n   199→    \"\"\"Extract Optional[Callable] constructor parameters.\n   200→\n   201→    Returns list of (class_name.param_name, param_name, line_number).\n   202→    \"\"\"\n   203→    injectables: list[tuple[str, str, int]] = []\n   204→\n   205→    for \"\"\"Check if an annotation represents Optional[Callable] or Callable | None.\"\"\"\n   234→    # Optional[Callable] -> Subscript(Name('Optional'), Name('Callable'))\n   235→    if isinstance(annotation, ast.Subscript):\n   236→        if isinstance(annotation.value, ast.Name) and annotation.value.id == \"Optional\":\n   237→            slice_val = annotation.slice\n   238→            if isinstance(slice_val, ast.Name) and slice_val.id == \"Callable\":\n   239→                return True\n   240→            # Optional[Callable[...]]\n   241→            if isinstance(slice_val, ast.Subscript):\n   242→                if isinstance(slice_val.value, ast.Name) and slice_val.value.id == \"Callable\":\n   243→                    return True\n   244→        # slice_val = annotation.slice\n   248→                if isinstance(slice_val, ast.Name) and slice_val.id == \"Callable\":\n   249→                    return True\n   250→\n   251→    # Callable | None -> BinOp(Name('Callable'), BitOr, Constant(None))\n   252→    if isinstance(annotation, ast.BinOp) and isinstance(annotation.op, ast.BitOr):\n   253→        left, right = annotation.left, annotation.right\n   254→        if (isinstance(left, ast.Name) and left.id == \"Callable\" and\n   255→                isinstance(right, ast.Constant) and right.value is None):\n   256→            return True\n   257→        if (isinstance(right, ast.Name) and right.id == \"Callable\" and\n   258→                isinstance(left, ast.Constant) and left.value is None):\n   259→            return True\n   260→\n   261→    return False\n   262→\n   263→\n   264→def _has_none_default(func_def: ast.FunctionDef, target_arg: ast.arg) -> bool:\n   265→    \"\"\"Check if the given arg has a default value of None.\"\"\"\n   266→    args = func_def.args\n   267→    # defaults apply to the last N positional args\n   268→    all_args = args.args\n   269→    defaults = args.defaults\n   270→\n   271→    if not defaults:\n   272→        return False\n   273→\n   274→    # Map defaults to args (right-aligned)\n   275→    offset = len(all_args) - len(defaults)\n   276→    for i, arg in enumerate(all_args):\n   277→        if arg is target_arg:\n   278→            default_idx = i - offset\n   279→            if 0 <= default_idx < len(defaults):\n   280→                default = defaults[default_idx]\n   281→                return isinstance(default, ast.Constant) and default.value is None\n   282→    return False\n   283→\n   284→\n   285→def _find_wiring_call_sites(\n keyword.\"\"\"\n   291→    for tree in trees.values():\n   292→        for node in ast.walk(tree):\n   293→            if isinstance(node, ast.Call):\n   294→                # Check keyword -> bool:\n   304→    \"\"\"Check if a Call node is calling the specified class.\"\"\"\n   305→    func = call.func\n   306→    if isinstance(func, ast.Name) and func.id == class_name:\n   307→        return True\n   308→    if isinstance(func, ast.Attribute) and func.attr == class_name:\n   309→        return True\n   310→    return False\n   311→\n   312→\n   313→def analyze_unwired_callables(\n
