task_results: list[TaskResult],\n    remaining_task_ids: list[str] | None = None,\n    budget_remaining: int = 0,\n) -> AggregatedPhaseReport:\n    \"\"\"Aggregate individual TaskResults into a runner-constructed PhaseReport.\n\n    This function is the runner's authoritative source of task outcomes.\n    It does not rely on agent self-reporting.\n\n    Args:\n        phase_number: The phase number being aggregated.\n        task_results: List of TaskResult from execute_phase_tasks().\n        remaining_task_ids: Task IDs that were not attempted due to budget.\n\n    Returns:\n        AggregatedPhaseReport with computed counts and status.\n    \"\"\"\n    report = AggregatedPhaseReport(\n        phase_number=phase_number,\n        task_results=task_results,\n        remaining_task_ids=remaining_task_ids or [],\n        budget_remaining=budget_remaining,\n    )\n\n    report.tasks_total = len(task_results) + len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n    report.tasks_passed = sum(1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS)\n    report.tasks_failed = sum(1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.FAIL)\n    report.tasks_incomplete = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n    )\n    report.tasks_skipped = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED\n    )\n    report.tasks_not_attempted = len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n    report.total_turns_consumed = sum(r.turns_consumed for r in task_results)\n    report.total_duration_seconds = sum(r.duration_seconds for r in task_results)\n\n    return report\n\n\ndef check_budget_guard(ledger: TurnLedger | None) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"Pre-launch budget guard: returns a halt message if budget is insufficient.\n\n    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n    if the budget is too low to launch a subprocess.\n    \"\"\"\n    if ledger is None:\n        return None\n    if ledger.can_launch():\n        return None\n    return (\n        f\"Budget exhausted: {ledger.available()} turns remaining, \"\n        f\"minimum {ledger.minimum_allocation} required for launch\"\n    )\n\n\ndef execute_phase_tasks(\n    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n    *,\n    _subprocess_factory=None,\n) -> tuple[list[TaskResult], list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\n\n    Iterates over a task inventory, spawning one subprocess per task with\n    budget allocation from the TurnLedger. Returns task results and a list\n    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n\n    Args:\n        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n        config: Sprint configuration.\n        phase: The Phase being executed.\n        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n\n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n    \"\"\"\n    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n    remaining: list[str] = []\n\n    if not tasks:\n        return results, remaining\n\n    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Budget check: can we launch?\n        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\n            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\n            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\n            for t in tasks[i:]:\n                results.append(\n                    TaskResult(\n                        task=t,\n                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\n                        started_at=started_at,\n                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n                    )\n                )\n            break\n\n        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\n        if ledger is not None:\n            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\n\n        # Spawn subprocess for this task\n        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n        else:\n            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n\n        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Determine task status from exit code\n        if exit_code == 0:\n            status = TaskStatus.PASS\n        elif exit_code == 124:\n            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        else:\n            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n\n        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\n        if ledger is not None:\n            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\n            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\n            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\n            if actual > pre_allocated:\n                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\n            elif actual < pre_allocated:\n                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\n\n        results.append(\n            TaskResult(\n                task=task,\n                status=status,\n                turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=output_bytes,\n            )\n        )\n\n    return results, remaining\n\n\ndef _run_task_subprocess(\n    task: TaskEntry,\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n    \"\"\"Run a single task in a subprocess. Returns (exit_code, turns, output_bytes).\n\n    This is the real implementation that spawns a ClaudeProcess. For testing,\n    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Build a task-specific prompt\n    prompt = (\n        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n    )\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n    proc.config = config\n    proc.phase = phase\n    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n\n    _Base.__init__(\n        proc,\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n    )\n    proc.start()\n    proc.wait()\n    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)\n\n\ndef execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n\n    For each active phase:\n    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n    2. Start output monitor thread\n    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n    5. Record PhaseResult\n    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n        raise SystemExit(\n            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n        )\n\n    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n    signal_handler.install()\n\n    setup_debug_logger(config)\n    import logging as _logging\n\n    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n\n    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n\n    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n\n    tui.start()\n\n    # Startup orphan cleanup: remove stale isolation dirs from crashed previous runs\n    shutil.rmtree(config.results_dir / \".isolation\", ignore_errors=True)\n\n    # Execute all python-mode phases via preflight executor before the main loop.\n    # Removing this single call reverts to all-Claude behavior (R-051 rollback property).\n    # Lazy import breaks the preflight → executor → preflight circular import cycle.\n    from .preflight import execute_preflight_phases  # noqa: PLC0415\n\n    preflight_results = execute_preflight_phases(config)\n\n    try:\n        for phase in config.active_phases:\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Python-mode phases were already executed by preflight; skip here.\n            if phase.execution_mode == \"python\":\n                continue\n\n            # Skip-mode phases: record SKIPPED with no subprocess launched.\n            if phase.execution_mode == \"skip\":\n                _now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n                skip_result = PhaseResult(\n                    phase=phase,\n                    status=PhaseStatus.SKIPPED,\n                    exit_code=0,\n                    started_at=_now,\n                    finished_at=_now,\n                )\n                sprint_result.phase_results.append(skip_result)\n                logger.write_phase_result(skip_result)\n                continue\n\n            # Per-phase isolation directory: exactly one file (the phase file)\n            isolation_dir = config.results_dir / \".isolation\" / f\"phase-{phase.number}\"\n            isolation_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            shutil.copy2(phase.file, isolation_dir / phase.file.name)\n\n            try:\n                # Reset monitor for this phase\n                output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n                monitor.reset(output_path)\n                monitor.start()\n\n                # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n                if config.tmux_session_name:\n                    update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n\n                # Launch claude with isolation env vars (CLAUDE_WORK_DIR → isolation_dir)\n                _phase_env_vars = {\n                    \"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\": str(isolation_dir),\n                }\n                proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase, env_vars=_phase_env_vars)\n                proc_manager.start()\n                started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n                # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n                deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n                logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n\n                debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n\n                tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n\n                # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n                # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n                _timed_out = False\n                _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n                _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n                while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n                    if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                        proc_manager.terminate()\n                        break\n                    if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n                        # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n                        _timed_out = True\n                        proc_manager.terminate()\n                        break\n\n                    ms = monitor.state\n                    _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"poll_tick\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                        poll_result=\"running\",\n                        elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n                        output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n                        growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                        stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n                    )\n\n                    # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n                    if (\n                        config.stall_timeout > 0\n                        and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n                        and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n                        and not _stall_acted\n                    ):\n                        _stall_acted = True\n                        debug_log(\n                            _dbg,\n                            \"watchdog_triggered\",\n                            phase=phase.number,\n                            action=config.stall_action,\n                            stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                            pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                        )\n                        if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n                            import sys\n\n                            print(\n                                f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                                f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n                                file=sys.stderr,\n                            )\n                            _timed_out = True\n                            proc_manager.terminate()\n                            break\n                        else:\n                            # warn action: log and continue\n                            import sys\n\n                            print(\n                                f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                                f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n                                file=sys.stderr,\n                            )\n\n                    # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n                    if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n                        _stall_acted = False\n\n                    # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n                    # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n                    try:\n                        tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n                    except Exception as _tui_exc:\n                        import sys\n\n                        print(\n                            f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n                    time.sleep(0.5)\n\n                # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n                # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n                raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n                if _timed_out:\n                    exit_code = 124\n                else:\n                    exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n                monitor.stop()\n                finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n                _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n                debug_log(\n                    _dbg,\n                    \"PHASE_END\",\n                    phase=phase.number,\n                    exit_code=exit_code,\n                    duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n                )\n\n                # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n                # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n                # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n                # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    logger.write_phase_interrupt(\n                        phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code\n                    )\n                    sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                    break\n\n                # Write a preliminary result sentinel so _determine_phase_status()\n                # always finds a result file for exit_code=0 phases that wrote no report.\n                # Guard: only for successful exits; non-zero paths must not reach this.\n                if exit_code == 0:\n                    _wrote_preliminary = _write_preliminary_result(\n                        config, phase, started_at.timestamp()\n                    )\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"preliminary_result_write\",\n                        path=f\"{'executor-preliminary (option_d)' if _wrote_preliminary else 'agent-written/option_a_or_noop'}\",\n                    )\n\n                # Determine phase status\n                status = _determine_phase_status(\n                    exit_code=exit_code,\n                    result_file=config.result_file(phase),\n                    output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n                    config=config,\n                    phase=phase,\n                    started_at=started_at.timestamp(),\n                    error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n                )\n\n                # Write executor result file for downstream consumers.\n                # Written AFTER status determination to avoid circularity.\n                # Overwrites any agent-written file — executor is authoritative.\n                _write_executor_result_file(\n                    config=config,\n                    phase=phase,\n                    status=status,\n                    exit_code=exit_code,\n                    monitor_state=monitor.state,\n                    started_at=started_at,\n                    finished_at=finished_at,\n                )\n\n                # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n                error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n                error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n\n                phase_result = PhaseResult(\n                    phase=phase,\n                    status=status,\n                    exit_code=exit_code,\n                    started_at=started_at,\n                    finished_at=finished_at,\n                    output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n                    error_bytes=error_bytes,\n                    last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n                    files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n                )\n                sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n\n                debug_log(\n                    _dbg,\n                    \"phase_complete\",\n                    phase=phase.number,\n                    status=status.value,\n                    exit_code=exit_code,\n                    duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n                )\n\n                # Log and notify\n                logger.write_phase_result(phase_result)\n                notify_phase_complete(phase_result)\n\n                tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, None)\n\n                # Decide: continue or halt?\n                if status.is_failure:\n                    # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n                    try:\n                        collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n                        bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n                        classifier = FailureClassifier()\n                        bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n                        reporter = ReportGenerator()\n                        diag_path = (\n                            config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n                        )\n                        reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n                        debug_log(\n                            _dbg,\n                            \"diagnostic_report\",\n                            phase=phase.number,\n                            category=bundle.category.value,\n                            path=str(diag_path),\n                        )\n                    except Exception as _diag_exc:\n                        debug_log(\n                            _dbg,\n                            \"diagnostic_error\",\n                            phase=phase.number,\n                            error=str(_diag_exc),\n                        )\n\n                    sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n                    sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n                    break\n\n            finally:\n                shutil.rmtree(isolation_dir, ignore_errors=True)\n\n        # Merge preflight results with main-loop results in original phase order.\n        # Build a lookup from phase number → PhaseResult, main-loop results win\n        # on conflict (they are the authoritative executor record for claude phases).\n        _merged: dict[int, PhaseResult] = {r.phase.number: r for r in preflight_results}\n        for r in sprint_result.phase_results:\n            _merged[r.phase.number] = r\n        sprint_result.phase_results = [\n            _merged[p.number] for p in config.active_phases if p.number in _merged\n        ]\n\n        # Sprint finished\n        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n            # Verify all phases actually passed\n            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n\n        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n\n    finally:\n        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n        try:\n            monitor.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        if proc_manager is not None:\n            try:\n                proc_manager.terminate()\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n        try:\n            tui.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        try:\n            signal_handler.uninstall()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n    try:\n        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n    except OSError:\n        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n\n    if _exitcode != 0:\n        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n\n\ndef _classify_from_result_file(\n    result_file: Path,\n    started_at: float,\n) -> PhaseStatus | None:\n    \"\"\"Classify phase outcome from the agent-written result file.\n\n    Returns a PhaseStatus if the result file exists, is fresh (mtime > started_at),\n    and contains a recognizable EXIT_RECOMMENDATION. Returns None if the file is\n    missing, stale, or unreadable.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not result_file.exists():\n        return None\n    try:\n        mtime = result_file.stat().st_mtime\n    except OSError:\n        return None\n    if started_at > 0 and mtime < started_at:\n        # Stale file from a previous run — do not trust\n        return None\n    try:\n        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    except OSError:\n        return None\n    upper = content.upper()\n    if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper:\n        return PhaseStatus.HALT\n    if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper:\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_RECOVERED\n    if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_RECOVERED\n    if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n        return PhaseStatus.HALT\n    if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n        return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n    return None\n\n\ndef _check_checkpoint_pass(config: SprintConfig, phase: Phase) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Return True if the end-of-phase checkpoint file exists with status PASS.\"\"\"\n    checkpoint_path = (\n        config.release_dir / \"checkpoints\" / f\"CP-P{phase.number:02d}-END.md\"\n    )\n    if not checkpoint_path.exists():\n        return False\n    try:\n        content = checkpoint_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\").upper()\n        return \"STATUS: PASS\" in content or \"**RESULT**: PASS\" in content\n    except OSError:\n        return False\n\n\ndef _check_contamination(config: SprintConfig, phase: Phase) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Return list of artifact files containing cross-phase task ID patterns.\"\"\"\n    import re as _re\n\n    contaminated: list[str] = []\n    artifacts_dir = config.release_dir / \"artifacts\"\n    if not artifacts_dir.exists():\n        return contaminated\n    next_phase = phase.number + 1\n    pattern = _re.compile(rf\"T{next_phase:02d}\\.\\d{{2}}\", _re.IGNORECASE)\n    for md_file in artifacts_dir.rglob(\"*.md\"):\n        try:\n            if pattern.search(md_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")):\n                contaminated.append(str(md_file.relative_to(config.release_dir)))\n        except OSError:\n            pass\n    return contaminated\n\n\ndef _write_crash_recovery_log(\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase: Phase,\n    contaminated: list[str],\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Append crash recovery entry to results/crash_recovery_log.md.\"\"\"\n    log_path = config.results_dir / \"crash_recovery_log.md\"\n    entry = (\n        f\"\\n## Phase {phase.number} — PASS_RECOVERED Recovery\\n\"\n        f\"**Timestamp**: {datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()}\\n\"\n        f\"**Checkpoint**: checkpoints/CP-P{phase.number:02d}-END.md (PASS)\\n\"\n        f\"**Contamination check**: \"\n        + (\n            \"CLEAN\"\n            if not contaminated\n            else f\"WARNING — {len(contaminated)} file(s): {contaminated}\"\n        )\n        + \"\\n\"\n        \"**Action**: Phase reclassified ERROR→PASS_RECOVERED.\\n\"\n    )\n    try:\n        with open(log_path, \"a\") as f:\n            f.write(entry)\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n\n\ndef _write_preliminary_result(\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase: Phase,\n    started_at: float,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Write a preliminary result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE sentinel.\n\n    This function is a deterministic fallback ensuring ``_determine_phase_status()``\n    always finds a valid result file for phases that exit 0 but write no report.\n    Without it, such phases return ``PASS_NO_REPORT`` because no result file is present.\n\n    **Ordering invariant**: Call *after* ``finished_at`` is captured and *before*\n    ``_determine_phase_status()`` is called. If called after status determination,\n    the sentinel file will not affect the already-computed status.\n\n    **Concurrency**: Assumes single-threaded execution (one phase at a time). If the\n    sprint loop is parallelised in future, this function must be replaced with an\n    ``O_EXCL``-based atomic write to prevent TOCTOU races between the exists-check\n    and the write.\n\n    **Sentinel contract**: Writes ``EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\\\n`` so that\n    ``_determine_phase_status()`` branch 3 (``result_file.exists()`` → CONTINUE check\n    at line 1024) returns ``PhaseStatus.PASS`` instead of falling through to the\n    ``PASS_NO_REPORT`` branch (line 1045). This sentinel is intentionally minimal;\n    the executor result file written by ``_write_executor_result_file()`` afterwards\n    will overwrite it with the authoritative structured report.\n\n    Args:\n        config: Sprint configuration providing ``result_file(phase)`` path.\n        phase: The phase whose result file to write.\n        started_at: Phase start time as a POSIX timestamp (``datetime.timestamp()``).\n            Used for the freshness guard: if a result file already exists with\n            ``st_mtime >= started_at`` and ``st_size > 0``, it is assumed to be a\n            valid agent-written file and is left untouched (no-op).\n\n    Returns:\n        ``True`` if the sentinel file was written, ``False`` if the file was preserved\n        (freshness guard triggered) or an ``OSError`` prevented the write. The boolean\n        is intended for telemetry logging only; callers should not branch on it.\n    \"\"\"\n    import logging as _logging\n\n    result_path = config.result_file(phase)\n    # Freshness guard: if a valid, fresh agent-written file is present, do not overwrite.\n    if result_path.exists():\n        try:\n            st = result_path.stat()\n            if st.st_size > 0 and st.st_mtime >= started_at:\n                # Fresh, non-empty agent file — preserve it\n                return False\n        except OSError:\n            pass  # Treat stat failure as absent; fall through to write\n\n    # Write the sentinel: zero-byte, stale, or missing files are all overwritten.\n    try:\n        result_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        result_path.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n        return True\n    except OSError as exc:\n        _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(\n            \"preliminary result write failed: %s; phase may report PASS_NO_REPORT\",\n            exc,\n        )\n        return False\n\n\ndef _write_executor_result_file(\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase: Phase,\n    status: PhaseStatus,\n    exit_code: int,\n    monitor_state: MonitorState,\n    started_at: datetime,\n    finished_at: datetime,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write executor-sourced result file for downstream consumers.\n\n    This is written AFTER _determine_phase_status returns, so it does not\n    create circularity. It provides a deterministic result file even when\n    the agent failed to write one.\n    \"\"\"\n    duration = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n    recommendation = \"CONTINUE\" if status.is_success else \"HALT\"\n    content = (\n        \"---\\n\"\n        f\"phase: {phase.number}\\n\"\n        f\"status: {'PASS' if status.is_success else 'FAIL'}\\n\"\n        f\"tasks_total: 1\\n\"\n        f\"tasks_passed: {1 if status.is_success else 0}\\n\"\n        f\"tasks_failed: {0 if status.is_success else 1}\\n\"\n        \"---\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        f\"# Phase {phase.number} — Executor Result Report\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        f\"| Phase | Status | Exit Code | Duration |\\n\"\n        f\"|-------|--------|-----------|----------|\\n\"\n        f\"| {phase.number} | {status.value} | {exit_code} | {duration:.1f}s |\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        f\"**Source**: executor (not agent self-report)\\n\"\n        f\"**Output bytes**: {monitor_state.output_bytes}\\n\"\n        f\"**Last task ID**: {monitor_state.last_task_id or 'n/a'}\\n\"\n        f\"**Files changed**: {monitor_state.files_changed}\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        f\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: {recommendation}\\n\"\n    )\n    result_path = config.result_file(phase)\n    try:\n        result_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        result_path.write_text(content)\n    except OSError:\n        pass  # Non-fatal — best effort\n\n\ndef _determine_phase_status(\n    exit_code: int,\n    result_file: Path,\n    output_file: Path,\n    *,\n    config: SprintConfig | None = None,\n    phase: Phase | None = None,\n    started_at: float = 0.0,\n    error_file: Path | None = None,\n) -> PhaseStatus:\n    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n\n    Priority:\n    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n    \"\"\"\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        # Path 1 — Specific: context exhaustion (Spec B S2)\n        # detect_prompt_too_long reads NDJSON output for \"Prompt is too long\"\n        if detect_prompt_too_long(output_file, error_path=error_file):\n            # Check if the agent managed to write a result file before exhaustion\n            result_status = _classify_from_result_file(result_file, started_at)\n            if result_status is not None:\n                return result_status\n            # No valid result file — context exhausted without completing\n            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n\n        # Path 2 — General: checkpoint inference (Spec A SOL-C)\n        # Reads agent-written checkpoint files (pre-crash evidence)\n        if config is not None and phase is not None:\n            if _check_checkpoint_pass(config, phase):\n                contaminated = _check_contamination(config, phase)\n                _write_crash_recovery_log(config, phase, contaminated)\n                if not contaminated:\n                    return PhaseStatus.PASS_RECOVERED\n\n        # Path 3 — Default: unchanged\n        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    if result_file.exists():\n        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n        upper = content.upper()\n        # Sentinel contract: EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE is written by\n        # _write_preliminary_result() as a deterministic fallback for phases that\n        # exit 0 but produce no agent result file. The presence of this sentinel\n        # here causes the phase to return PASS rather than fall through to\n        # PASS_NO_REPORT (branch 4). _write_executor_result_file() overwrites this\n        # file after status determination with the authoritative structured report.\n        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n        if has_halt:\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        if has_continue:\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n","numLines":1154,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1154}}}

194→\n   195→\n   196→def _extract_injectable_params(\n   197→    tree: ast.Module, file_path: str\n   198→) -> list[tuple[str, str, int]]:\n   199→    \"\"\"Extract Optional[Callable] constructor \"\"\"Check if an annotation represents Optional[Callable] or Callable | None.\"\"\"\n   234→    # Optional[Callable] -> Subscript(Name('Optional'), Name('Callable'))\n   235→    if isinstance(annotation, ast.Subscript):\n   236→        if isinstance(annotation.value, ast.Name) and annotation.value.id == \"Optional\":\n   237→            slice_val = annotation.slice\n   238→            if isinstance(slice_val, ast.Name) and slice_val.id == \"Callable\":\n   239→                return True\n   240→            # Optional[Callable[...]]\n   241→            if isinstance(slice_val, ast.Subscript):\n   242→                if isinstance(slice_val.value, ast.Name) and slice_val.value.id == \"Callable\":\n   243→                    return True\n   244→        # Also check Attribute form: typing.Optional\n   245→        if isinstance(annotation.value, ast.Attribute):\n   246→            if annotation.value.attr == \"Optional\":\n   247→                slice_val = annotation.slice\n   248→                if isinstance(slice_val, ast.Name) and slice_val.id == \"Callable\":\n   249→                    return True\n   250→\n   251→    # Callable | None -> BinOp(Name('Callable'), BitOr, Constant(None))\n   252→    if isinstance(annotation, ast.BinOp) and isinstance(annotation.op, ast.BitOr):\n   253→        left, right = annotation.left, annotation.right\n   254→        if (isinstance(left, ast.Name) and left.id == \"Callable\" and\n   255→                isinstance(right, ast.Constant) and right.value is None):\n   256→            return True\n   257→        if (isinstance(right, ast.Name) and right.id == \"Callable\" and\n   258→                isinstance(left, ast.Constant) and left.value is None):\n   259→            return True\n   260→\n   261→    return False\n   262→\n   263→\n   264→def _has_none_default(func_def: ast.FunctionDef, target_arg: ast.arg) -> bool:\n   265→    \"\"\"Check if the given arg has a default value of None.\"\"\"\n   266→    args = func_def.args\n   267→    # defaults apply to the last N positional args\n   268→    all_args = args.args\n   269→    defaults = args.defaults\n   270→\n   271→    if not defaults:\n   272→        return False\n   273→\n   274→    # Map defaults to args (right-aligned)\n   275→    offset = len(all_args) - len(defaults)\n   276→    for i, arg in enumerate(all_args):\n   277→        if arg is target_arg:\n   278→            default_idx = i - offset\n   279→            if 0 <= default_idx < len(defaults):\n   280→                default = defaults[default_idx]\n   281→                return isinstance(default, ast.Constant) and default.value is None\n   282→    return False\n   283→\n   284→\n   285→def _find_wiring_call_sites(\n keyword.\"\"\"\n   291→    for tree in trees.values():\n   292→        for node in ast.walk(tree):\n   293→            if isinstance(node, ast.Call):\n   294→                # Check keyword 319→    Algorithm (section 5.2.1):\n   320→    1. Apply exclude patterns\n   440→            if any(fnmatch(py_file.name, pat) for pat in config.exclude_patterns):\n   441→                continue\n   442→\n   443→            # Derive the module path components\n   444→            try:\n   445→                rel = py_file.relative_to(source_dir)\n   446→            except ValueError:\n   447→                continue\n   448→\n   449→            # Check if any external file imports this module\n   454→            is_imported = False\n   455→            for imp in all_imports:\n   456→                if module_dotpath in imp or module_stem in imp:\n   457→                    is_imported = True\n   458→                    break\n   459→\n   460→            if not is_imported:\n   461→                findings.append(\n   462→                    WiringFinding(\n   463→                        finding_type=\"orphan_module\",\n   464→                        file_path=str(py_file),\n   465→                        symbol_name=module_dotpath,\n   466→                        line_number=1,\n   467→                        detail=(\n   468→                            f\"Module '{module_dotpath}' in provider directory \"\n   469→                            f\"'{provider_dir.name}' has zero inbound imports\"\n   470→                        ),\n   471→                        severity=\"major\",\n   472→                    )\n   473→                )\n   474→\n   475→    # Check module-level definitions\n   495→    for node in ast.iter_child_nodes(tree):\n   496→        if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef)):\n   497→            if node.name == name:\n   498→                return True\n   499→        elif isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef):\n   500→            if node.name == name:\n   501→                return True\n   502→        elif isinstance(node, ast.Assign):\n   503→            for target in node.targets:\n   504→                if isinstance(target, ast.Name) and target.id == name:\n   505→                    return True\n   506→\n   507→    # Check if importable\n   508→    if name in all_imports:\n   509→        return True\n   510→\n   511→    return False\n   512→\n   513→\n   514→def analyze_registries(\n   515→    config: WiringConfig,\n   516→    source_dir: Path,\n   517→) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n   518→    \"\"\"Detect broken dispatch registry entries referencing nonexistent callables.\n   519→\n   520→    Algorithm (section 5.2.3):\n   521→    1. Scan files for module-level dict assignments matching registry patterns\n   522→    2. For each dict entry, verify the value references an importable callable\n   523→    3. Unresolved references emit unwired_registry findings\n   524→\n   525→    Args:\n   526→        config: Wiring analysis configuration.\n   527→        source_dir: Root directory to scan.\n   528→\n   529→    Returns:\n   530→        List of WiringFinding with finding_type=\"unwired_registry\".\n   531→    \"\"\"\n   532→    files, _ = _collect_python_files(source_dir, config.exclude_patterns)\n   533→    trees: dict[Path, ast.Module] = {}\n   534→    all_imports_by_file: dict[Path, set[str]] = {}\n   535→\n   536→    for f in files:\n   537→        tree = _safe_parse(f)\n   538→        if tree is not None:\n   539→            trees[f] = tree\n   540→            all_imports_by_file[f] = _extract_imports(tree)\n   541→\n   542→    findings: list[WiringFinding] = []\n   543→\n   544→    for file_path, tree in trees.items():\n   545→        file_imports = all_imports_by_file.get(file_path, set())\n   546→\n   547→        for node in ast.iter_child_nodes(tree):\n   548→            if not isinstance(node, ast.Assign):\n   549→                continue\n   550→\n   551→            for target in node.targets:\n   552→                if not isinstance(target, ast.Name):\n   553→                    continue\n   554→                if not _matches_registry_pattern(target.id, config.registry_patterns):\n   555→                    continue\n   556→\n   557→                # This is a registry assignment — check if value is a dict\n   558→                if not isinstance(node.value, ast.Dict):\n   559→                    continue\n   560→\n   561→                for key_node, val_node in zip(node.value.keys, node.value.values):\n   562→                    if val_node is None:\n   563→                        continue\n   564→\n   565→                    # Explicit None is allowed\n   566→                    if isinstance(val_node, ast.Constant) and val_node.value is None:\n   567→                        continue\n   568→\n   569→                    # Name reference — must be resolvable\n   570→                    if isinstance(val_node, ast.Name):\n   571→                        if not _resolve_name_in_scope(\n   572→                            val_node.id, tree, file_imports\n   573→                        ):\n   574→                            key_repr = (\n   575→                                ast.literal_eval(key_node)\n   576→                                if isinstance(key_node, ast.Constant)\n   577→                                else \"<dynamic>\"\n   578→                            )\n   579→                            findings.append(\n   580→                                WiringFinding(\n   581→                                    finding_type=\"unwired_registry\",\n   582→                                    file_path=str(file_path),\n   583→                                    symbol_name=f\"{target.id}[{key_repr!r}]\",\n   584→                                    line_number=val_node.lineno,\n   585→                                    detail=(\n   586→                                        f\"Registry '{target.id}' entry \"\n   587→                                        f\"'{key_repr}' references unresolvable \"\n   588→                                        f\"callable '{val_node.id}'\"\n   589→                                    ),\n   590→                                    severity=\"critical\",\n   591→                                )\n   592→                            )\n   593→\n   594→                    # String reference — try to resolve as dotted path\n   595→                    elif isinstance(val_node, ast.Constant) and isinstance(\n   596→                        val_node.value, str\n   597→                    ):\n   598→                        ref = val_node.value\n   599→                        parts = ref.rsplit(\".\", 1)\n   600→                        func_name = parts[-1] if len(parts) > 1 else ref\n   601→                        if not _resolve_name_in_scope(\n   602→                            func_name, tree, file_imports\n   603→                        ):\n   604→                            key_repr = (\n   605→                                ast.literal_eval(key_node)\n   606→                                if isinstance(key_node, ast.Constant)\n   607→                                else \"<dynamic>\"\n   608→                            )\n   609→                               detail=(\n   616→                                        f\"Registry '{target.id}' entry \"\n   617→                                        f\"'{key_repr}' references severity=\"critical\",\n   621→                                )\n   622→                            )\n   623→\n   624→    # Apply whitelist\n   625→    whitelist = ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   630→# Top-level entry point\n   631→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   632→\n   633→\n   634→def run_wiring_analysis(\n   635→    config: WiringConfig,\n   636→    source_dir: Path,\n   637→) -> WiringReport:\n   638→    \"\"\"Run all three wiring analyzers and produce a WiringReport.\n   639→\n   640→    Args:\n   641→        config: Wiring analysis configuration.\n   642→        source_dir: Root directory to scan.\n   643→\n   644→    Returns:\n   645→        WiringReport aggregating findings from all analyzers.\n   646→    \"\"\"\n   647→    start = time.monotonic()\n   648→\n   649→    _, skipped = _collect_python_files(source_dir, config.exclude_patterns)\n   650→    files, _ = _collect_python_files(source_dir, config.exclude_patterns)\n   651→    files_analyzed = len(files)\n   652→\n   653→    unwired = analyze_unwired_callables(config, source_dir)\n   654→    orphans = analyze_orphan_modules(config, source_dir)\n   655→    registries = analyze_registries(config, source_dir)\n   656→\n   657→    duration = time.monotonic() - start\n   658→\n   659→    return WiringReport(\n   660→        target_dir=str(source_dir),\n   661→        files_analyzed=files_analyzed,\n   662→        files_skipped=skipped,\n   663→        unwired_callables=unwired,\n   664→        orphan_modules=orphans,\n   665→        unwired_registries=registries,\n   666→        scan_duration_seconds=round(duration, 4),\n   667→        rollout_mode=config.rollout_mode,\n   668→    )\n   669→\n   670→\n   671→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   672→# Report emission (section 5.4)\n   673→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   674→\n   675→\n   676→def emit_report(report: WiringReport, output_path: Path) -> Path:\n   677→    \"\"\"Write a wiring verification report with YAML frontmatter and 7 Markdown sections.\n   678→\n   679→    Frontmatter contains 16 fields per section 5.6. String fields are serialized\n   680→    with yaml.safe_dump() to prevent YAML injection (section 5.4).\n   681→\n   682→    Args:\n   683→        report: Aggregated wiring analysis report.\n   684→        output_path: Destination file path.\n   685→\n   686→    Returns:\n   687→        The output_path after writing.\n   688→    \"\"\"\n   689→    # Compute severity counts from unsuppressed findings\n   690→    unsuppressed = report.unsuppressed_findings\n   691→    critical_count = sum(1 for f in unsuppressed if f.severity == \"critical\")\n   692→    major_count = sum(1 for f in unsuppressed if f.severity == \"major\")\n   693→    info_count = sum(1 for f in unsuppressed if f.severity == \"info\")\n   694→    suppressed_count = sum(1 for f in report.all_findings if f.suppressed)\n   695→\n   696→    # blocking_findings depends on rollout_mode\n   697→    blocking = report.blocking_count()\n   698→\n   699→    # Build frontmatter dict (16 fields per section 5.6)\n   700→    fm: dict = {\n   701→        \"gate\": \"wiring-verification\",\n   702→        \"target_dir\": report.target_dir,\n   703→        \"files_analyzed\": report.files_analyzed,\n   704→        \"files_skipped\": report.files_skipped,\n   705→        \"rollout_mode\": report.rollout_mode,\n   706→        \"analysis_complete\": True,\n   707→        \"audit_artifacts_used\": 0,\n   708→        \"unwired_callable_count\": len([\n   709→            f for f in report.unwired_callables if not f.suppressed\n   710→        ]),\n   711→        \"orphan_module_count\": len([\n   712→            f for f in report.orphan_modules if not f.suppressed\n   713→        ]),\n   714→        \"unwired_registry_count\": len([\n   715→            f for f in report.unwired_registries if not f.suppressed\n   716→        ]),\n   717→        \"critical_count\": critical_count,\n   718→        \"major_count\": major_count,\n   719→        \"info_count\": info_count,\n   720→        \"total_findings\": len(unsuppressed),\n   721→        \"blocking_findings\": blocking,\n   722→        \"whitelist_entries_applied\": suppressed_count,\n   723→    }\n   724→\n   725→    # Serialize frontmatter with yaml.safe_dump for string safety\n   726→    fm_yaml = yaml.safe_dump(fm, default_flow_style=False, sort_keys=False)\n   727→\n   728→    # Build Markdown body with 7 sections (section 5.4.1)\n   729→    sections: list[str] = []\n   730→\n   731→    # 1. Summary\n   732→    sections.append(\"## Summary\\n\")\n   733→    sections.append(\n   734→        f\"- **Target**: {report.target_dir}\\n\"\n   735→        f\"- **Files analyzed**: {report.files_analyzed}\\n\"\n   736→        f\"- **Files skipped**: {report.files_skipped}\\n\"\n   737→        f\"- **Rollout mode**: {report.rollout_mode}\\n\"\n   738→        f\"- **Total findings**: {len(unsuppressed)}\\n\"\n   739→        f\"- **Blocking findings**: {blocking}\\n\"\n   740→        f\"- **Scan duration**: {report.scan_duration_seconds:.4f}s\\n\"\n   741→    )\n   742→\n   743→    # 2. Unwired Optional Callable Injections\n   744→    sections.append(\"## Unwired Optional Callable Injections\\n\")\n   745→    uc = [f for f in report.unwired_callables if not f.suppressed]\n   746→    if uc:\n   747→        for f in uc:\n   748→            sections.append(\n   749→                f\"- **{f.symbol_name}** ({f.file_path}:{f.line_number}) \"\n   750→                f\"[{f.severity}]: {f.detail}\\n\"\n   751→            )\n   752→    else:\n   753→        sections.append(\"No unsuppressed unwired callable findings.\\n\")\n   754→\n   755→    # 3. Orphan Modules / Symbols\n   756→    sections.append(\"## Orphan Modules / Symbols\\n\")\n   757→    om = [f for f in report.orphan_modules if not f.suppressed]\n   758→    if om:\n   759→        for f in om:\n   760→            sections.append(\n   761→                f\"- **{f.symbol_name}** ({f.file_path}:{f.line_number}) \"\n   762→                f\"[{f.severity}]: {f.detail}\\n\"\n   763→            )\n   764→    else:\n   765→        sections.append(\"No unsuppressed orphan module findings.\\n\")\n   766→\n   767→    # 4. Unregistered Dispatch Entries\n   768→    sections.append(\"## Unregistered Dispatch Entries\\n\")\n   769→    ur = [f for f in report.unwired_registries if not f.suppressed]\n   770→    if ur:\n   771→        for f in ur:\n   772→            sections.append(\n   773→                f\"- **{f.symbol_name}** ({f.file_path}:{f.line_number}) \"\n   774→                f\"[{f.severity}]: {f.detail}\\n\"\n   775→            )\n   776→    else:\n   777→        sections.append(\"No unsuppressed registry findings.\\n\")\n   778→\n   779→    # 5. Suppressions and Dynamic Retention\n   780→    sections.append(\"## Suppressions and Dynamic Retention\\n\")\n   781→    suppressed_findings = [f for f in report.all_findings if f.suppressed]\n   782→    if suppressed_findings:\n   783→        for f in suppressed_findings:\n   784→            sections.append(\n   785→                f\"- **{f.symbol_name}** ({f.finding_type}): suppressed\\n\"\n   786→            )\n   787→    else:\n   788→        sections.append(\"No suppressions applied.\\n\")\n   789→    sections.append(f\"- Audit artifacts used: 0\\n\")\n   790→\n   791→    # 6. Recommended Remediation\n   792→    sections.append(\"## Recommended Remediation\\n\")\n   793→    if uc:\n   794→        sections.append(\n   795→            \"- **Unwired callables**: Wire Optional[Callable] parameters \"\n   796→            \"at call sites or add to whitelist if intentionally unused.\\n\"\n   797→        )\n   798→    if om:\n   799→        sections.append(\n   800→            \"- **Orphan modules**: Import orphan modules from consumer code \"\n   801→            \"or remove if no longer needed.\\n\"\n   802→        )\n   803→    if ur:\n   804→        sections.append(\n   805→            \"- **Broken registries**: Fix registry entries to reference \"\n   806→            \"resolvable callables or remove stale entries.\\n\"\n   807→        )\n   808→    if not uc and not om and not ur:\n   809→        sections.append(\"No remediation needed — all checks pass.\\n\")\n   810→\n   811→    # 7. Evidence and Limitations\n   812→    sections.append(\"## Evidence and Limitations\\n\")\n   813→    sections.append(\n   814→        \"- Analysis uses AST-based static analysis; dynamic imports and \"\n   815→        \"runtime wiring are not detected.\\n\"\n   816→        \"- Alias resolution is limited to direct name references.\\n\"\n   817→        \"- Registry detection uses pattern matching on variable names; \"\n   818→        \"non-conventional naming may be missed.\\n\"\n   819→    )\n   820→\n   821→    # Assemble full document\n   822→    body = \"\\n\".join(sections)\n   823→    content = f\"---\\n{fm_yaml}---\\n\\n{body}\"\n   824→\n   825→    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   826→    output_path.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   827→    return output_path\n   828→\n   829→\n   830→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   831→# Frontmatter extraction (section 5.5, NFR-007 compliant)\n   832→# \"\"\"Determine whether findings should block the pipeline based on rollout_mode.\n   997→\n   998→    Blocking truth table (section 5.1):\n   999→    - shadow: always False (findings logged, never
